name: The Mossen Place — Hardby Safe House

text.content:
The Mossen Place

Hardby merchant-quarter side street. A three-storey townhome owned covertly by Caelith Dunivar. The party's base of operations for Phase 3 (and a returning resource for any later phase that brings them back to Hardby).

How the party learns about it

Caelith hands the lead PC a small folded paper at the Loftwick farewell in Phase 2 — the same morning he gives them the brass Hand-of-the-Duke token. The paper has, in his careful handwriting:

17 Cooper's Tale, Hardby merchant quarter. Ask Mistress Halver at the corner grocer for the key. Tell her the cellarer's apprentice came down from Loftwick to mind the house. She will not ask further. Make yourselves at home; it has been some time since the chimneys were swept, but everything else is sound.

The house is in my name only at one remove. The deed is held by “Mistress Eleyn Mossen, retired, of Hardby.” She does not exist. Use her name as you would a tool. Do not let her name attach to yours in writing.

You may rest there. You may receive visitors there. You may store there what you would not store at a Hardby inn. If the Yeomanry asks me about the property in formal session, I will not know what they mean.

— C.

The cover identity

Mistress Eleyn Mossen — a fictitious retired widow Caelith has maintained through a discreet Loftwick solicitor (Mistress Aldaen Veth) for nearly a decade. The solicitor pays Hardby rates and tithes on the property each quarter via courier; the property is, in surface terms, completely above-board. "Mistress Mossen" has never been seen, but her signature appears (in Caelith's own hand) on every renewal of the property's papers.

The cover is robust. Hardby Watch has no record of the house being anything other than the home of an absentee elderly widow. Cindren & Vhal's local branch has no reason to be interested in 17 Cooper's Tale; the firm does not handle non-bonded private property.

What works

  • Cistern. The roof cistern is full from autumn rains. Cold water at the kitchen sink and the upper-floor baths.
  • Hearths and chimneys. All three flues sweep clean; the parlor, dining-room, and kitchen fires can be lit on arrival.
  • Locks. Front door, back service door, and the upper-floor bedrooms all have functional locks. Caelith's spare key is the one Mistress Halver hands over; PCs can have copies made at any Hardby locksmith.
  • Pantry. Stocked with non-perishables — tea, dried beans, hard biscuits, salt fish, oil and vinegar, a wax-sealed crock of honey. Mistress Halver's grocer delivers a small quarterly restock under a standing order Caelith arranged.
  • Linen and towels. Cedar-stored in the third-floor linen closet; smells faintly of last year's cedar oil; usable.
  • Furniture. Sound but covered in white linen DUST-SHEETS in the parlor, dining room, and bedrooms. A morning's work to uncover, beat clean, and resettle.

What doesn't (yet)

  • Dust. Everywhere. A thin patina on flat surfaces, cobwebs in upper corners, dust-sheets needing to be taken outside and beaten. A morning of work for a full party; an afternoon if one PC does it alone.
  • Fresh food. The pantry's non-perishables are sound but the party will need to shop for produce, milk, and meat at the merchant-quarter market.
  • Lamps. Half the lamps need fresh wicks; a few of the oil-pans need topping up. Mistress Halver sells both.
  • Garden. The herb bed in the back yard has gone wild — only rosemary, sage, and parsley have survived. A Survival or Nature DC 12 identifies what's worth keeping.
  • Smell. A faintly musty closed-house smell on arrival; gone within a day of open windows.

The two hidden caches

Caelith has left two prepared caches in the house. Neither is mentioned in the folded paper.

Cache 1 — The Library Compartment (Second Floor, M2)

Under the floorboards of the second-floor library, beside the writing desk on the east outer wall, a SMALL HIDDEN COMPARTMENT.

  • Investigation DC 13 on the library floor finds a slightly raised edge where one floorboard sits ~1/16 inch proud of its neighbors.
  • Sleight of Hand DC 10 or Thieves' Tools DC 12 lifts the board cleanly.
  • Contents: an iron-bound lockbox (Thieves' Tools DC 14 to open if the party hasn't found the key) containing:
    • A small jar of GOOD LAMP OIL (six refills).
    • A QUIRE of BLANK PAPER and three sticks of SEALING WAX in different colours (red, black, dark blue).
    • Four FOLDED HARDBY STREET-MAPS, hand-drawn, showing the merchant quarter, the docks, the C&V branch's block, and the Gynarchy Registry quarter.
    • A SEALED LETTER (red wax, Caelith's chop) addressed simply: “To whoever finds this.”
  • The key to the lockbox is hidden under the wash-stand basin in M4 Bath (Perception DC 14).

Cache 2 — The Linen-Closet Floorboard (Third Floor, T6)

Under the floorboards of the third-floor linen closet, in the centre of the small storage room, a SECOND HIDDEN COMPARTMENT containing emergency funds.

  • Investigation DC 14 on the closet floor finds the loose board (a slightly different shading, a slightly faster ring when tapped).
  • Contents: a small canvas purse holding 200 gp in mixed coin (Aerdi imperial, Yeomanry trade, Hardby civic). The purse is unmarked.
  • A small folded note inside the purse, in Caelith's hand: “Do not be proud about taking this. Replace what you can when you can. — C.”

Caelith's letter

Verbatim, the sealed letter in the library cache:

Friends —

If you are reading this, you have found the floorboard. I assumed you would. The compartment is small and you are not stupid.

Three things, briefly.

First — this house is yours for as long as you need it. I will not ask what you do here. I would prefer it if you did not write to me from it under your own names; the four maps in the lockbox are routes that have, at various times, worked.

Second — the Hardby branch of Cindren & Vhal is at 4 Brass Lane, four streets east of Cooper's Tale. The branch is open from second bell after dawn until fifth bell after noon. The bonded clerk on duty most days is a thin man named Jaron Pell; he is paid to be civil to walk-ins and to send a runner to Rel Astra at the first sign of meaningful inquiry. Veska Maelan at the trade-clearing notary's office on Long Quay will be more useful to you than the firm itself.

Third — if any of you is hurt in a way that the Hardby Watch should not see, take them to Brother Wenra Holst at the small infirmary on Cobbler's Walk (three streets south of here). He is a half-orc cleric of Pelor who treats discreetly for a donation. I have left twenty gold with him against your account; it is enough for two serious mendings or one extended convalescence.

I have not left a weapon in the house. I have left a place to put a weapon down.

Make use of the kitchen. Do not let the cistern run dry; I have asked Mistress Halver to top it from the well if you forget. Do not, under any circumstance, sign the Hardby property roll with a name you wish to keep your own.

I will see you in Loftwick or I will not.

— C.

The grocer — Mistress Halver

Mistress Edda Halver — sixties, widowed, runs the corner grocer at the south end of Cooper's Tale. Knows the house has belonged to an absentee elderly widow she has never seen. Knows nothing about Caelith, Loftwick, or the Yeomanry. Hands over the key when the party speaks the right phrase ("the cellarer's apprentice came down from Loftwick to mind the house"). She is pleasant, incurious, and a useful local source if treated kindly — she knows the merchant quarter's gossip down to the half-hour.

If asked about "Mistress Mossen," Mistress Halver will say only, "I have not had occasion to meet her. The accounts arrive by courier from Loftwick, as they always have. The house is paid up." She is telling the truth.

Use cases for the party

  • Lay low between confrontations. Especially after any visible interaction with the C&V Hardby branch.
  • Receive Veska Maelan privately — the trade-clearing notary; she will not come to an inn but she will come here if asked discreetly.
  • Receive Castrian Vell — Trina's poet; he prefers the dining room (he is sentimental about long oak tables).
  • Store recovered evidence. The library cache compartment, augmented by the party's own caching arrangements.
  • Plan operations against the C&V Hardby underwriter or the broker Tarsh, using the maps in the cache.
  • Convalescence. If a PC is hurt, the master bedroom on the second floor is the largest and quietest.

What this house is NOT

  • It is NOT defensible. A determined attack with four or more attackers and a battering ram takes the door in under a minute. The interior is timber-framed; arrows pass through walls. If the party expects assault, they should leave or fortify (and fortification will attract Watch attention).
  • It is NOT a long-term hideout. Caelith maintains the cover by paying tithes on time and by ensuring the grocer's restock continues; sustained occupancy beyond two or three weeks will draw neighbourhood notice ("isn't Mistress Mossen back at last? She has visitors"). Phase 3 fits comfortably inside that window.
  • It is NOT free of all observation. The neighbours at 15 and 19 Cooper's Tale will notice lamp-light, smoke from the chimneys, and any visitors. None of them are conspirators; they are simply attentive Hardby townspeople. A loud party in the dining room WILL be remarked upon.

Ground Floor — Layout

Street side at the bottom of the map (front door on Cooper's Tale); back yard at the top of the map (alley behind).

G1 — Front Foyer

Small entry just inside the front door. A tall coat-rail on the east wall with a single forgotten dark wool cloak hanging on it (no name in the collar; Caelith may know, but does not say). An umbrella stand. Open archway north into the central hall.

G2 — Parlor

West of the central hall. Two facing settees under white linen dust-sheets — soft dusty cream and pale rose visible at the hems. A low oak table between them; a film of dust visible on its surface. A small fireplace on the west outer wall (cold; old ash on the grate; flue sweeps clean). A small bookcase against the south interior wall with a dozen books: poetry, a Hardby civic almanac (three years out of date), a Greyhawk gazetteer, a moralising treatise on bonded credit (uncut pages). Two leaded-glass windows on the south (street) outer wall.

G3 — Stair Hall

East of the central hall. Contains the wooden quarter-turn staircase up to the second floor. The stair starts at the north end of the room and rises along the east wall before turning south at a small landing. A small wash-stand with a porcelain basin at the foot of the stair (the basin is empty and dust-filmed; the brass tap above it works). One door from the central hall on the west wall.

G4 — Kitchen

The north end of the building, full width. Stone-tiled floor (cool underfoot). A brick hearth on the west outer wall with hanging copper pots (a few have visible green patina; serviceable after a polish). A sturdy wooden worktable in the centre — the worktable has one clean spot wiped on its surface, where someone (the GM should decide whether this was Mistress Halver dusting last week, or a previous occupant) cleaned a small area without bothering with the rest. A stone sink under a small window in the north outer wall; the brass tap works; cold water from the cistern. A service door in the east wall opens directly onto the back yard.

G5 — Scullery / Pantry

A small annex on the east side of the kitchen, north of the service door. Shelves of dry goods: three sacks of dried beans, a small barrel of oats, a few jars of pickled vegetables, a wax-sealed crock of honey, hanging cured meat (one ham, one shoulder of bacon, both wax-papered), tea in a tin, a small barrel of vinegar, a small jar of salt. A stoneware washing basin in the corner. One open archway connects to the kitchen.

G6 — Back Yard

Walled courtyard north of the building. Stone flag paving. A small herb bed along the east wall (mostly gone wild — rosemary, sage, and parsley have survived; Survival DC 12 identifies). A stone bench against the west wall. A small wooden privy (outhouse) tucked into the north-west corner — usable; the pit was last cleaned within the year per the smell. A stone well-head with a wooden cover in the centre of the yard — the well draws clean (Mistress Halver tops the cistern from it). A small wrought-iron pedestrian gate in the north wall opens onto the back alley (a quiet service alley running the length of Cooper's Tale, used by night-soil collectors and the grocer's apprentice).

Doors and movement

  • Front door (south outer wall): solid dark oak, brass knocker, single deadbolt lock (Sleight of Hand or Thieves' Tools DC 12). The brass knocker is tarnished.
  • Foyer ↔ Central Hall: open archway, no door leaf.
  • Central Hall ↔ Parlor: single hinged door, west.
  • Central Hall ↔ Stair Hall: single hinged door, east.
  • Central Hall ↔ Kitchen: single hinged door, north end.
  • Kitchen ↔ Scullery: open archway, no door leaf.
  • Kitchen ↔ Back Yard: solid wooden service door, east wall, single deadbolt (DC 12).
  • Back Yard ↔ Back Alley: wrought-iron pedestrian gate, padlocked from inside (DC 11; Caelith's key fits).

Second Floor — Layout

The street side is at the bottom of the map (with two leaded-glass windows over the parlor below); the back of the house is at the top (master bedroom's two windows look down on the back yard).

M1 — Dining Room

West of the central hall, mirroring the parlor below. A long polished oak dining table (8 ft × 3 ft, seats 8), with eight wooden chairs around it — each chair draped with a white linen dust-sheet, so they read as draped white shapes from above. A sideboard against the north interior wall with empty silver candle-holders (well-tarnished). Two leaded-glass windows on the south (street) outer wall, with a small wrought-iron balcony rail just outside (decorative; not a usable balcony — you cannot stand on it without removing the windows).

M2 — Library / Study

East of the central hall, in the middle of the building. Bookcases along the east outer wall and the south interior wall (floor-to-ceiling). A writing desk against the east wall under a small window. A chair pulled out from the desk. A reading chair in the corner. The bookcases hold a mix:

  • A complete Yeomanry Civil Code (three volumes; tooled green leather; dust on the top of the volumes but not on their spines).
  • Aerdi commercial treatises (six volumes; sober brown).
  • A small shelf of poetry — Castrian Vell's first volume Roads I Did Not Take, signed to "E.M." (Eleyn Mossen — or, in fact, no one).
  • A Hardby gazetteer.
  • A few practical-arts manuals.

The library compartment. Under the floorboards beside the writing desk, on the east outer wall side: a small hidden compartment with an iron-bound lockbox (see Overview page for contents). Investigation DC 13 finds the slightly-raised floorboard.

M4 — Bath / Water Closet

East of the stair hall. A tin tub set on a low wooden platform. A wash-stand with a porcelain basin. A hand-pump for water above the basin — works; cold only. A small wooden privy stool over a sealed drain that runs to the yard cesspit. A wall-mirror with cracked silvering. One door from the stair hall on the west wall.

M3 — Master Bedroom

The north end of the building, full width × 12 ft deep. A generous four-poster bed against the north outer wall, with the bed and its hangings draped in a single large white linen dust-sheet (so the bed reads as a tall draped white mass with four posts visible at the corners). A tall wardrobe against the west wall (empty; smells of cedar). A small wash-stand with a porcelain basin against the east wall (empty; brass tap works). A small low table beside the bed. Two small leaded-glass windows on the north outer wall (looking down at the back yard).

The master bedroom is the largest in the house. The party should decide who sleeps here; it could be Cam (whose halfling network thread often brings late-night visitors he'd rather entertain alone), or Elle (whose order's quiet morning office wants quiet space), or it could be left as a shared sitting-room and the party all sleep on the third floor.

M5 — Central Stair Hall

The east-center of the floor. The staircase from the ground floor arrives at the south end of this room (along the east wall). A second staircase rises from this room up to the third floor, at the north end (along the east wall). Both staircases visible. One door on the west wall opens to the central hall; one door on the east wall opens to the bath M4; one door on the north wall (just past the upward staircase) leads to the central hall continuing north toward the master bedroom.

Doors and movement

  • Stair Hall ↔ Dining Room: open archway through the central hall, single hinged door at the dining room entry.
  • Stair Hall ↔ Central N-S Hall: open archway, north end.
  • Stair Hall ↔ Bath: single hinged door, east.
  • Central Hall ↔ Library: single hinged door, east.
  • Central Hall ↔ Master Bedroom: single hinged door, north end.
  • No outer-wall doors on this floor. The dining-room windows are sealed leaded glass (cannot be used as exits without breaking).

Third Floor — Layout

The dormer level, under the slate roof. Four small bedrooms in the corners (one in each), a central corridor, a small bath, and a linen / storage closet with the second hidden cache.

T1 — T4 PC Bedrooms (four)

Each bedroom is small (about 10 ft × 10 ft for T1/T2, slightly larger 10 ft × 12 ft for T3/T4 at the back), with:

  • A single narrow bed against an outer wall, with a white linen dust-sheet draped over it.
  • A small wardrobe.
  • A wash-stand with a porcelain basin (empty; brass tap works).
  • A dormer window cut into the sloped roof, projecting outward from the wall.
  • A door onto the central corridor.

The rooms are cozy rather than grand — historically these were servant or guest rooms. They are comfortable for the party to sleep in without anyone feeling crowded, but no PC will spend a leisurely afternoon in their own bedroom here.

T5 — Small Bath

At the north end of the central corridor, between T3 and T4. A wash-basin and a chamber-pot screen. Less generous than M4 on the second floor. Used by whichever PCs are bunking up here.

T6 — Linen / Storage Closet

A small closet off the central corridor, south of the bath. Shallow shelves hold:

  • Folded sheets and pillowcases (cedar-stored; usable).
  • A few woollen blankets.
  • A small stack of old candles in a tin.
  • A small box of dried lavender bundles (faded but still fragrant).
  • A neatly folded apron and a feather duster (Mistress Halver's, left here from the last cleaning).

The linen-closet cache. Under the floorboards in the center of this closet, a hidden compartment with the second cache (see Overview page for contents). Investigation DC 14 finds the loose board. Tip: a PC who taps the closet floor (Perception DC 13) hears a hollower note in one square.

T7 — Stair Landing

The south end of the floor, where the staircase from the second floor arrives. The landing is small — just enough to turn safely. The central corridor runs north from the landing.

Doors and movement

  • Stair Landing ↔ T1 Bedroom One (south-west): single hinged door, west.
  • Stair Landing ↔ T2 Bedroom Two (south-east): single hinged door, east.
  • Stair Landing ↔ North Corridor: open archway, north.
  • Corridor ↔ T3 Bedroom Three (north-west): single hinged door, west.
  • Corridor ↔ T4 Bedroom Four (north-east): single hinged door, east.
  • Corridor ↔ T5 Bath: single hinged door, north end.
  • Corridor ↔ T6 Linen Closet: single hinged door, middle.
  • NO outer-wall doors. All windows are small leaded dormer windows in the sloped roof — usable as exits only by very small or athletic PCs (Athletics DC 14 to climb out + dexterity save to land on the slate without sliding).

Roof access

There is no formal roof-access hatch on this floor. A determined PC can break out through a dormer window (small humans tight squeeze; halflings comfortable) onto the slate roof. The roof slopes sharply on both sides; a balancing pole or rope is recommended. From the roof's peak the party can see across to 15 and 19 Cooper's Tale (their neighbours' chimneys), and beyond, the upper storeys of the C&V Hardby branch four streets east.

pages.name: GM Notes — Using the Safe House

text.content:
GM Notes — Using the Safe House

Pacing tip — the dusting scene

The first day at the Mossen Place is great for a small downtime scene. The party arrives, Mistress Halver hands over the key, they let themselves in to a quiet musty house, and they spend an evening uncovering furniture and lighting fires. This is the moment for:

  • The forgotten cloak in the foyer. Whose was it? Caelith does not say. A PC who pockets it and produces it later may get a quiet reaction from Caelith in a future scene.
  • The wiped spot on the kitchen worktable. Mistress Halver's most recent visit, or something else? Insight DC 13 on Mistress Halver: she has not been inside in three months, except to top the cistern.
  • Finding the library compartment. Investigation DC 13. The party reads Caelith's letter that evening.
  • The neighbour at 15 Cooper's Tale. A quiet older man (Doral Hurst, retired Hardby Watch sergeant) waves from his front door if a PC steps outside. Tells the party, friendly: "You're the cellarer's apprentice? Good. The old place has been too quiet too long. Mistress Halver has the run of it usually, but it wanted noise." Friendly; not suspicious; nominally watchful but not in any way conspiring. He will notice everything that happens at 17 Cooper's Tale going forward.

The grocer's gossip

If a PC chats with Mistress Halver after the first day, she will share — if asked — what she has heard about the C&V Hardby branch in the last few months:

  • The bonded clerk on duty most days is Jaron Pell (the man Caelith's letter named). She thinks he is unhappy in his work.
  • A senior partner has come down from Rel Astra twice in the last six weeks, both times for only a day each. She does not have a name; she heard the senior partner described as "the one who never lets anyone see his hands." GM-only: this is Tarlith Vhal Sereth.
  • A man matching Merev Sarth's intermediary Tarsh's description has been seen drinking at the Coopered Wreck tavern on Long Quay. ("Thin, fifties, voice like a smoker's. Drinks alone. Always sits with his back to the wall. I don't trust men who do that — my husband used to do that.")
  • The grain factor Tamsin Moraven came through Hardby fifteen days ago; Mistress Halver saw her get off a wagon at the eastern caravan yard. "She looked tired. She didn't see me. I waved." She does not know what became of Tamsin after that. This is a major Phase 3 lead.

Brother Wenra Holst

The half-orc cleric of Pelor that Caelith's letter mentioned. Treats discreetly at his small infirmary on Cobbler's Walk, three streets south. Holds 20 gp of Caelith's credit; will accept further donations against future visits. Honest, kind, exhausted; the Hardby Watch knows him as a charitable physician and does not ask questions. This is a useful resource for the party throughout Phase 3 and beyond — he can stabilise, heal, and (with a donation) cast lesser restoration once per long rest.

What happens if the party is loud

  • Loud combat inside the house — Doral Hurst at #15 sends his teenage grandson to fetch the Watch within 5 minutes.
  • Loud music or large dinner party — #15 and #19 both notice; #15 quietly tells Mistress Halver, who quietly tells no one (she likes the party).
  • Bringing a visible Cindren & Vhal staff member here — an absolute disaster; the cover is permanently broken for any future use. Caelith will be coldly disappointed.
  • Visible Watch attention (e.g. the Watch knocks at the door asking after a fight elsewhere) — the party should answer politely as "the cellarer's apprentice and friends, minding the house for Mistress Mossen." A simple Deception or Persuasion DC 12 holds; a failed roll prompts the Watch to ask follow-up questions that will be uncomfortable but not catastrophic.

If a hostile faction discovers the house

This is a Phase 4+ problem rather than a Phase 3 problem, but if the party is careless — e.g. if they let a captured route-cleaner escape, or if they openly tell Hesren Vesh's contacts where they're staying — the conspirators may eventually identify the Mossen Place as theirs.

If that happens:

  • The deed-paper trail is robust enough that Cindren & Vhal cannot legally seize the house. The cover holds against formal scrutiny.
  • But the conspirators can set the house on fire at any time, and they may. Caelith will be quietly furious but not surprised.
  • If the house is destroyed, the two hidden caches survive in their respective compartments (the floorboards over the compartments are slightly more fire-resistant than the surrounding floor; not by accident).
  • Replacement of the safe house is not possible in Phase 3 or 4. Phase 5+ may give Caelith a chance to set up another.

Wiring

  • Scene: Mossen Place — Ground Floor (Phase 3 Scenes; wall + door + light coverage shipped).
  • Scene: Mossen Place — Second Floor (Phase 3 Scenes).
  • Scene: Mossen Place — Third Floor (Phase 3 Scenes).
  • Cross-references: Phase 2 — Departure (Caelith hands over the key in his farewell scene); Phase 3 — Arrival (the party arrives at Cooper's Tale on their first morning); Phase 3 — Hardby NPCs (Mistress Halver, Doral Hurst, Brother Wenra Holst, Jaron Pell are listed there).
